mechanism: Measure correlation between two tasks across sampled individuals by systematically varying structural features of tasks while holding psychological process (intent) constant. Compare individual-difference correlations under different structural correspondence levels to assess whether measurement format explains apparent divergence.
distribution: outcome: correlation structure across individuals; assumed: normal individual differences

mechanism: Decompose goal progress information (completed vs. remaining) and model motivation as a function of inferred commitment (when uncertain) or progress deficit (when certain). Predict interaction between commitment certainty and framing across multiple behavioral contexts.
distribution: motivation as conditional on commitment state and frame

mechanism: Compare stated preferences (self-report on hypothetical ideal partners) against revealed preferences in live interaction via correlation and meta-analysis across 17 dependent variables measuring relationship initiation. Test whether a priori theories diverge from actual behavior.

Measure processing depth (thought favorability and argument sensitivity) as a function of value importance linked to message content. Test mediation: value importance increases elaboration, which increases resistance via stronger cognitive foundations.
distribution: outcome: attitude extremity and thought favorability; assumed: individual differences normal

mechanism: Vary frame emphasis (to-date vs. to-go) of commitment state and measure motivation responses across goal types differing in certainty. Predict interaction: uncertain commitment responds to accomplishment signals; certain commitment responds to progress deficit signals.
distribution: motivation conditioned on commitment type and frame
